message.content:
Implement the following plan:

# E2E Test Harness for External Agents

## Context

The external-agents repo has only unit tests. We need a true E2E harness that builds agent binaries and exercises them via their CLI interface (subcommands with JSON stdin/stdout), the same way the Entire CLI invokes them in production. This mirrors the pattern from `devenv/cli/cmd/entire/cli/integration_test/` but adapted for standalone agent binaries.

## Design Decisions

| Decision | Rationale |
|----------|-----------|
| Top-level `e2e/` directory | Centralizes all E2E tests, shared harness code |
| Separate Go module (`e2e/go.mod`) | Avoids import-coupling with agent modules; tests only invoke compiled binaries |
| `//go:build e2e` tag | Keeps E2E tests out of `go test ./...`; requires `-tags=e2e` |
| Auto-discovery of agents in TestMain | Adding `agents/entire-agent-foo/` auto-includes it in build step |
| `ENTIRE_REPO_ROOT` env var for isolation | Already read by `protocol.RepoRoot()` (protocol.go:73-76) |
| `HOME` override to temp dir | Isolates SQLite/IDE path lookups from real user data |

## Files to Create

### 1. `e2e/go.mod`
```
module github.com/entireio/external-agents/e2e
go 1.26.0
```
No external dependencies — stdlib only.

### 2. `e2e/setup_test.go` — TestMain (binary builder)
- Auto-discover all `agents/entire-agent-*` directories
- Build each binary once into a shared temp dir via `go build`
- Store paths in package-level `agentBinaries` map
- Clean up temp dir after all tests complete

### 3. `e2e/harness.go` — AgentRunner (generic binary invoker)
Core struct with methods:
- `Run(stdin, subcommand, args...) CommandResult` — raw execution
- `RunJSON(t, dest, stdin, subcommand, args...)` — execute + JSON decode stdout
- `MustSucceed(t, stdin, subcommand, args...)` — assert exit code 0
- `MustFail(t, stdin, subcommand, args...)` — assert non-zero exit

`CommandResult` holds `Stdout []byte`, `Stderr []byte`, `ExitCode int`, `Err error`.

### 4. `e2e/testenv.go` — TestEnv (environment setup)
Layered factories:
- `NewTestEnv(t, agentName)` — bare temp dir with `ENTIRE_REPO_ROOT` + isolated `HOME`
- `NewKiroTestEnv(t)` — adds `.kiro/` and `.entire/tmp/` directories
- `NewKiroGitEnv(t)` — adds `git init`

Helper methods: `WriteFile`, `WriteJSON`, `ReadFile`, `FileExists`, `MkdirAll`, `GitInit`, `AbsPath`.

### 5. `e2e/fixtures.go` — Input builders
- `HookInput` struct with `.JSON()` method for building stdin payloads
- `KiroTranscript` builder with fluent API: `NewKiroTranscript(id).AddPrompt("...").AddPromptWithFileEdit("...", path).AddResponse("...", "...").JSON()`

### 6. `e2e/kiro_test.go` — Full protocol coverage for Kiro
Tests for all 19 subcommands, organized by functional area:

| Area | Subcommands | Test Count |
|------|-------------|------------|
| Identity | `info`, `detect` (present + absent) | 3 |
| Session management | `get-session-id`, `get-session-dir`, `resolve-session-file`, `read-session`, `write-session` | 5 |
| Transcript | `read-transcript`, `chunk-transcript`, `reassemble-transcript` | 3 |
| Hooks | `parse-hook` (spawn, prompt-submit, pre-tool-use, stop), `install-hooks`, `uninstall-hooks`, `are-hooks-installed` | 6 |
| Transcript analysis | `get-transcript-position`, `extract-modified-files`, `extract-prompts`, `extract-summary` | 4 |
| Other | `format-resume-command`, error cases | 3 |

All tests use `t.Parallel()` and `t.TempDir()`.

### 7. Top-level `Makefile` (new)
```makefile
test-e2e:
    cd e2e && go test -tags=e2e -v -count=1 ./...

test-unit:
    @for dir in agents/entire-agent-*/; do $(MAKE) -C $$dir test; done

test-all: test-unit test-e2e
```

## Adding a New Agent

When someone creates `agents/entire-agent-foo/`:
1. TestMain auto-discovers and builds it — no registration needed
2. Create `e2e/foo_test.go` with `//go:build e2e`
3. Optionally add `NewFooTestEnv(t)` factory if agent needs specific dirs (e.g., `.foo/`)
4. Run `make test-e2e`

## Critical Files

| File | Why |
|------|-----|
| `agents/entire-agent-kiro/cmd/entire-agent-kiro/main.go` | Subcommand list — tests must cover all 19 |
| `agents/entire-agent-kiro/internal/protocol/protocol.go` | Handler signatures — defines flag names, stdin/stdout contracts |
| `agents/entire-agent-kiro/internal/protocol/types.go` | JSON response shapes — tests decode these |
| `agents/entire-agent-kiro/internal/kiro/agent.go` | Agent implementation — understand behavior to write assertions |
| `agents/entire-agent-kiro/internal/kiro/hooks.go` | Hook file paths — tests verify file-system side effects |
| `agents/entire-agent-kiro/internal/kiro/transcript.go` | Transcript format — drives fixture builder design |

## Verification

1. `cd e2e && go test -tags=e2e -v -count=1 ./...` — all tests pass
2. Each subcommand from main.go has at least one test
3. Tests run in parallel with no flakiness (temp dir isolation)
4. Adding a dummy `agents/entire-agent-test/` triggers auto-build in TestMain


If you need specific details from before exiting plan mode (like exact code snippets, error messages, or content you generated), read the full transcript at: /Users/alisha/.claude/projects/-Users-alisha-Projects-external-agents/df1f566d-c6a7-4e90-8a83-550905805878.jsonl

### 1. `e2e/go.mod`

`★ Insight ─────────────────────────────────────`
This E2E harness follows a classic pattern for testing CLI tools: build the binary once in `TestMain`, then invoke it as a subprocess in each test. This gives true integration coverage — no mocking, no import coupling — just the same stdin/stdout contract that production uses.
`─────────────────────────────────────────────────`

file.content:
package main

import (
	"fmt"
	"os"

	"github.com/entireio/external-agents/agents/entire-agent-kiro/internal/kiro"
	"github.com/entireio/external-agents/agents/entire-agent-kiro/internal/protocol"
)

func main() {
	agent := kiro.New()

	if len(os.Args) < 2 {
		fatalf("usage: entire-agent-kiro <subcommand> [args]")
	}

	var err error

	switch os.Args[1] {
	case "info":
		err = protocol.WriteJSON(os.Stdout, agent.Info())
	case "detect":
		err = protocol.WriteJSON(os.Stdout, agent.Detect())
	case "get-session-id":
		err = protocol.HandleGetSessionID(os.Stdin, os.Stdout, agent)
	case "get-session-dir":
		err = protocol.HandleGetSessionDir(os.Args[2:], os.Stdout, agent)
	case "resolve-session-file":
		err = protocol.HandleResolveSessionFile(os.Args[2:], os.Stdout, agent)
	case "read-session":
		err = protocol.HandleReadSession(os.Stdin, os.Stdout, agent)
	case "write-session":
		err = protocol.HandleWriteSession(os.Stdin, agent)
	case "read-transcript":
		err = protocol.HandleReadTranscript(os.Args[2:], os.Stdout, agent)
	case "chunk-transcript":
		err = protocol.HandleChunkTranscript(os.Args[2:], os.Stdin, os.Stdout, agent)
	case "reassemble-transcript":
		err = protocol.HandleReassembleTranscript(os.Stdin, os.Stdout, agent)
	case "format-resume-command":
		err = protocol.HandleFormatResumeCommand(os.Args[2:], os.Stdout, agent)
	case "parse-hook":
		err = protocol.HandleParseHook(os.Args[2:], os.Stdin, os.Stdout, agent)
	case "install-hooks":
		err = protocol.HandleInstallHooks(os.Args[2:], os.Stdout, agent)
	case "uninstall-hooks":
		err = agent.UninstallHooks()
	case "are-hooks-installed":
		err = protocol.WriteJSON(os.Stdout, protocol.AreHooksInstalledResponse{
			Installed: agent.AreHooksInstalled(),
		})
	case "get-transcript-position":
		err = protocol.HandleGetTranscriptPosition(os.Args[2:], os.Stdout, agent)
	case "extract-modified-files":
		err = protocol.HandleExtractModifiedFiles(os.Args[2:], os.Stdout, agent)
	case "extract-prompts":
		err = protocol.HandleExtractPrompts(os.Args[2:], os.Stdout, agent)
	case "extract-summary":
		err = protocol.HandleExtractSummary(os.Args[2:], os.Stdout, agent)
	default:
		fatalf("unknown subcommand: %s", os.Args[1])
	}

	if err != nil {
		fatalf("%v", err)
	}
}

func fatalf(format string, args ...any) {
	_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, format+"\n", args...)
	os.Exit(1)
}

content.text: The build command needs to run from the agent's own module directory. Let me fix the build logic:
The issue is that each agent has its own `go.mod`, so `go build` needs to run from the agent's directory. Let me fix the build logic:

input.old_string:
for _, agentDir := range agents {
		agentName := filepath.Base(agentDir)
		binPath := filepath.Join(tmpDir, agentName)
		mainPkg := filepath.Join(agentDir, "cmd", agentName)

		fmt.Printf("Building %s...\n", agentName)
		cmd := exec.Command("go", "build", "-o", binPath, "./"+mainPkg)
		cmd.Dir = repoRoot()
		cmd.Stdout = os.Stdout
		cmd.Stderr = os.Stderr
		if err := cmd.Run(); err != nil {
			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "failed to build %s: %v\n", agentName, err)
			os.Exit(1)
		}

		agentBinaries[agentName] = binPath
		fmt.Printf("Built %s -> %s\n", agentName, binPath)
	}
input.new_string:
for _, agentDir := range agents {
		agentName := filepath.Base(agentDir)
		binPath := filepath.Join(tmpDir, agentName)
		// Each agent has its own go.mod, so build from the agent's directory
		agentAbsDir := filepath.Join(repoRoot(), agentDir)
		mainPkg := "./cmd/" + agentName

		fmt.Printf("Building %s...\n", agentName)
		cmd := exec.Command("go", "build", "-o", binPath, mainPkg)
		cmd.Dir = agentAbsDir
		cmd.Stdout = os.Stdout
		cmd.Stderr = os.Stderr
		if err := cmd.Run(); err != nil {
			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "failed to build %s: %v\n", agentName, err)
			os.Exit(1)
		}

		agentBinaries[agentName] = binPath
		fmt.Printf("Built %s -> %s\n", agentName, binPath)
	}

content.text:
All 29 tests pass via `make test-e2e`. 

`★ Insight ─────────────────────────────────────`
**Key design patterns in this harness:**
1. **TestMain as build orchestrator** — `discoverAgents()` scans `agents/entire-agent-*` directories, so adding a new agent automatically gets it built and available for testing. No registration needed.
2. **Process isolation via `ENTIRE_REPO_ROOT` + `HOME` override** — each test gets its own temp dir as the "repo root" and a separate temp HOME, so tests can run in parallel without stepping on each other's filesystem state (especially important for SQLite paths and hook files).
3. **Layered test environments** — `NewTestEnv` → `NewKiroTestEnv` → `NewKiroGitEnv` lets each test request exactly the setup it needs, keeping tests fast by not doing unnecessary work (e.g., `git init` only when needed).
`─────────────────────────────────────────────────`

Here's a summary of what was created:

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `e2e/go.mod` | Separate Go module — stdlib only, no import coupling |
| `e2e/setup_test.go` | `TestMain` that auto-discovers and builds all agent binaries |
| `e2e/harness.go` | `AgentRunner` with `Run`, `RunJSON`, `MustSucceed`, `MustFail` |
| `e2e/testenv.go` | `TestEnv` with layered factories (`NewTestEnv`, `NewKiroTestEnv`, `NewKiroGitEnv`) |
| `e2e/fixtures.go` | `HookInput`, `ParseHookInput`, `KiroTranscript` builders with fluent API |
| `e2e/kiro_test.go` | 29 tests covering all 19 subcommands across identity, sessions, transcript, hooks, transcript analysis, and error cases |
| `Makefile` | `test-e2e`, `test-unit`, `test-all` targets |

file.content:
# E2E Test Harness for External Agents

## Context

The external-agents repo has only unit tests. We need a true E2E harness that builds agent binaries and exercises them via their CLI interface (subcommands with JSON stdin/stdout), the same way the Entire CLI invokes them in production. This mirrors the pattern from `devenv/cli/cmd/entire/cli/integration_test/` but adapted for standalone agent binaries.

## Design Decisions

| Decision | Rationale |
|----------|-----------|
| Top-level `e2e/` directory | Centralizes all E2E tests, shared harness code |
| Separate Go module (`e2e/go.mod`) | Avoids import-coupling with agent modules; tests only invoke compiled binaries |
| `//go:build e2e` tag | Keeps E2E tests out of `go test ./...`; requires `-tags=e2e` |
| Auto-discovery of agents in TestMain | Adding `agents/entire-agent-foo/` auto-includes it in build step |
| `ENTIRE_REPO_ROOT` env var for isolation | Already read by `protocol.RepoRoot()` (protocol.go:73-76) |
| `HOME` override to temp dir | Isolates SQLite/IDE path lookups from real user data |

## Files to Create

### 1. `e2e/go.mod`
```
module github.com/entireio/external-agents/e2e
go 1.26.0
```
No external dependencies — stdlib only.

### 2. `e2e/setup_test.go` — TestMain (binary builder)
- Auto-discover all `agents/entire-agent-*` directories
- Build each binary once into a shared temp dir via `go build`
- Store paths in package-level `agentBinaries` map
- Clean up temp dir after all tests complete

### 3. `e2e/harness.go` — AgentRunner (generic binary invoker)
Core struct with methods:
- `Run(stdin, subcommand, args...) CommandResult` — raw execution
- `RunJSON(t, dest, stdin, subcommand, args...)` — execute + JSON decode stdout
- `MustSucceed(t, stdin, subcommand, args...)` — assert exit code 0
- `MustFail(t, stdin, subcommand, args...)` — assert non-zero exit

`CommandResult` holds `Stdout []byte`, `Stderr []byte`, `ExitCode int`, `Err error`.

### 4. `e2e/testenv.go` — TestEnv (environment setup)
Layered factories:
- `NewTestEnv(t, agentName)` — bare temp dir with `ENTIRE_REPO_ROOT` + isolated `HOME`
- `NewKiroTestEnv(t)` — adds `.kiro/` and `.entire/tmp/` directories
- `NewKiroGitEnv(t)` — adds `git init`

Helper methods: `WriteFile`, `WriteJSON`, `ReadFile`, `FileExists`, `MkdirAll`, `GitInit`, `AbsPath`.

### 5. `e2e/fixtures.go` — Input builders
- `HookInput` struct with `.JSON()` method for building stdin payloads
- `KiroTranscript` builder with fluent API: `NewKiroTranscript(id).AddPrompt("...").AddPromptWithFileEdit("...", path).AddResponse("...", "...").JSON()`

### 6. `e2e/kiro_test.go` — Full protocol coverage for Kiro
Tests for all 19 subcommands, organized by functional area:

| Area | Subcommands | Test Count |
|------|-------------|------------|
| Identity | `info`, `detect` (present + absent) | 3 |
| Session management | `get-session-id`, `get-session-dir`, `resolve-session-file`, `read-session`, `write-session` | 5 |
| Transcript | `read-transcript`, `chunk-transcript`, `reassemble-transcript` | 3 |
| Hooks | `parse-hook` (spawn, prompt-submit, pre-tool-use, stop), `install-hooks`, `uninstall-hooks`, `are-hooks-installed` | 6 |
| Transcript analysis | `get-transcript-position`, `extract-modified-files`, `extract-prompts`, `extract-summary` | 4 |
| Other | `format-resume-command`, error cases | 3 |

All tests use `t.Parallel()` and `t.TempDir()`.

### 7. Top-level `Makefile` (new)
```makefile
test-e2e:
    cd e2e && go test -tags=e2e -v -count=1 ./...

test-unit:
    @for dir in agents/entire-agent-*/; do $(MAKE) -C $$dir test; done

test-all: test-unit test-e2e
```

## Adding a New Agent

When someone creates `agents/entire-agent-foo/`:
1. TestMain auto-discovers and builds it — no registration needed
2. Create `e2e/foo_test.go` with `//go:build e2e`
3. Optionally add `NewFooTestEnv(t)` factory if agent needs specific dirs (e.g., `.foo/`)
4. Run `make test-e2e`

## Critical Files

| File | Why |
|------|-----|
| `agents/entire-agent-kiro/cmd/entire-agent-kiro/main.go` | Subcommand list — tests must cover all 19 |
| `agents/entire-agent-kiro/internal/protocol/protocol.go` | Handler signatures — defines flag names, stdin/stdout contracts |
| `agents/entire-agent-kiro/internal/protocol/types.go` | JSON response shapes — tests decode these |
| `agents/entire-agent-kiro/internal/kiro/agent.go` | Agent implementation — understand behavior to write assertions |
| `agents/entire-agent-kiro/internal/kiro/hooks.go` | Hook file paths — tests verify file-system side effects |
| `agents/entire-agent-kiro/internal/kiro/transcript.go` | Transcript format — drives fixture builder design |

## Verification

1. `cd e2e && go test -tags=e2e -v -count=1 ./...` — all tests pass
2. Each subcommand from main.go has at least one test
3. Tests run in parallel with no flakiness (temp dir isolation)
4. Adding a dummy `agents/entire-agent-test/` triggers auto-build in TestMain

1→# E2E Tests
     2→
     3→End-to-end tests for the `entire` CLI against real agents (Claude Code, Gemini CLI, OpenCode, Cursor, Factory AI Droid, Copilot CLI).
     4→
     5→## Commands
     6→
     7→```bash
     8→mise run test:e2e [filter]                          # run filtered (or omit filter for all agents)
     9→mise run test:e2e --agent claude-code [filter]       # Claude Code only
    10→mise run test:e2e --agent gemini-cli [filter]        # Gemini CLI only
    11→mise run test:e2e --agent opencode [filter]          # OpenCode only
    12→mise run test:e2e --agent cursor [filter]            # Cursor only
    13→mise run test:e2e --agent factoryai-droid [filter]   # Factory AI Droid only
    14→mise run test:e2e --agent copilot-cli [filter]       # Copilot CLI only
    15→go build ./...                                      # compile check (no agent CLI needed)
    16→```
    17→
    18→**Do NOT run E2E tests proactively.** They make real API calls that consume tokens and cost money. Only run when explicitly asked.
    19→
    20→## Structure
    21→
    22→```
    23→e2e/
    24→├── agents/       # Agent abstraction (Agent interface, tmux sessions, concurrency gates)
    25→├── bootstrap/    # CI pre-test setup (auth config, warmup)
    26→├── entire/       # `entire` CLI wrapper (enable, rewind, etc.)
    27→├── exploratory/  # Experimental tests, not run by CI
    28→├── tests/        # Blessed test files (run by CI)
    29→└── testutil/     # Repo setup, assertions, artifact capture
    30→```
    31→
    32→## Key Patterns
    33→
    34→- Every test uses `testutil.ForEachAgent` which runs it per registered agent with repo setup, concurrency gating, and timeout scaling.
    35→- All operations go through `RepoState` (`s.RunPrompt`, `s.Git`) so they're logged to `console.log`.
    36→- Use the `entire` package for CLI interactions, not raw `exec.Command`.
    37→- Skip tests pending CLI fixes with `t.Skip("ENT-XXX: reason")`.
    38→
    39→## Adding a New Agent
    40→
    41→1. Create `agents/<name>.go` implementing the `Agent` interface.
    42→2. Register it in `init()` with `Register(&YourAgent{})`.
    43→3. Add a `Bootstrap()` method for any CI-specific setup (auth config, warmup).
    44→4. Add a `RegisterGate("<name>", N)` call if concurrency needs limiting.
    45→5. Ensure the agent name is accepted by `mise run test:e2e --agent <name>`.
    46→6. Add the agent to `.github/workflows/e2e.yml` matrix and `e2e-isolated.yml` options.
    47→
    48→## Environment Variables
    49→
    50→| Variable | Description | Default |
    51→|----------|-------------|---------|
    52→| `E2E_AGENT` | Agent to test (`claude-code`, `gemini-cli`, `opencode`, `cursor`, `factoryai-droid`, `copilot-cli`) | all registered |
    53→| `E2E_ENTIRE_BIN` | Path to a pre-built `entire` binary | builds from source |
    54→| `E2E_TIMEOUT` | Timeout per prompt | `2m` |
    55→| `E2E_KEEP_REPOS` | Set to `1` to preserve temp repos after test | unset |
    56→| `E2E_ARTIFACT_DIR` | Override artifact output directory | `e2e/artifacts/<timestamp>` |
    57→| `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` | Required for Claude Code | — |
    58→| `GEMINI_API_KEY` | Required for Gemini CLI | — |
    59→| `COPILOT_GITHUB_TOKEN` | Required for Copilot CLI (or `gh auth login`) | — |
    60→
    61→## Debugging Failures
    62→
    63→Artifacts are captured to `e2e/artifacts/` on every run (git-log, git-tree, console.log, checkpoint metadata, entire logs). Set `E2E_KEEP_REPOS=1` to preserve the temp repo — a symlink appears in the artifact dir pointing to it.
    64→
    65→Use the `debug-e2e` skill (`.claude/skills/debug-e2e/`) for a structured workflow when investigating failures.
    66→
    67→### Reading artifacts
    68→
    69→- `console.log` — full operation transcript including agent stdout/stderr
    70→- `git-log.txt` — commit history at time of failure
    71→- `git-tree.txt` — working tree state
    72→- `entire-logs/` — internal CLI logs
    73→
    74→### Fixing flaky tests
    75→
    76→When a test passes on retry but failed once, the problem is usually agent non-determinism, not a CLI bug. Common patterns:
    77→
    78→- **Agent asked for confirmation instead of acting**: The model output contains "Does this look right?" or "Should I proceed?". Fix: append "Do not ask for confirmation, just make the change." to the prompt.
    79→- **Agent wrote to wrong path or created extra files**: Fix: be more explicit about exact file paths and what _not_ to do.
    80→- **Agent committed when it shouldn't have**: Fix: add "Do not commit" to the prompt.
    81→- **Checkpoint wait timeout**: `WaitForCheckpoint` or `WaitForCheckpointAdvanceFrom` exceeded deadline. Fix: increase the timeout argument.
    82→
    83→To diagnose: read `console.log` in the failing test's artifact directory. Compare what the agent actually did vs what the test expected.
    84→
    85→## CI Workflows
    86→
    87→- **`.github/workflows/e2e.yml`** — Runs full suite on push to main. Matrix: `[claude-code, opencode, gemini-cli, cursor-cli, factoryai-droid, copilot-cli]`.
    88→- **`.github/workflows/e2e-isolated.yml`** — Manual dispatch for debugging a single test. Inputs: agent + test name filter.
    89→
    90→Both workflows run `go run ./e2e/bootstrap` before tests to handle agent-specific CI setup (auth config, warmup).
    91→

1→//go:build e2e
     2→
     3→package tests
     4→
     5→import (
     6→	"context"
     7→	"testing"
     8→	"time"
     9→
    10→	"github.com/entireio/cli/e2e/testutil"
    11→	"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
    12→)
    13→
    14→// TestExternalAgentSingleSessionManualCommit: external agent creates a file,
    15→// user commits manually. Validates the full external agent protocol flow:
    16→// discovery via PATH, hook firing through binary subcommands, transcript
    17→// handling, and checkpoint creation.
    18→func TestExternalAgentSingleSessionManualCommit(t *testing.T) {
    19→	testutil.ForEachAgent(t, 2*time.Minute, func(t *testing.T, s *testutil.RepoState, ctx context.Context) {
    20→		_, err := s.RunPrompt(t, ctx,
    21→			"create a file called docs/hello.md")
    22→		if err != nil {
    23→			t.Fatalf("agent failed: %v", err)
    24→		}
    25→
    26→		testutil.AssertFileExists(t, s.Dir, "docs/hello.md")
    27→
    28→		s.Git(t, "add", ".")
    29→		s.Git(t, "commit", "-m", "Add hello file via external agent")
    30→
    31→		testutil.AssertNewCommits(t, s, 1)
    32→		testutil.WaitForCheckpoint(t, s, 15*time.Second)
    33→		testutil.AssertCheckpointAdvanced(t, s)
    34→
    35→		cpID := testutil.AssertHasCheckpointTrailer(t, s.Dir, "HEAD")
    36→		testutil.AssertCheckpointExists(t, s.Dir, cpID)
    37→		testutil.AssertCheckpointMetadataComplete(t, s.Dir, cpID)
    38→		testutil.AssertCheckpointHasSingleSession(t, s.Dir, cpID)
    39→		testutil.AssertCheckpointFilesTouchedContains(t, s.Dir, cpID, "docs/hello.md")
    40→		testutil.AssertNoShadowBranches(t, s.Dir)
    41→	})
    42→}
    43→
    44→// TestExternalAgentMultipleTurnsManualCommit: external agent handles two
    45→// sequential prompts, user commits once. Both prompts should be captured
    46→// in a single checkpoint.
    47→func TestExternalAgentMultipleTurnsManualCommit(t *testing.T) {
    48→	testutil.ForEachAgent(t, 2*time.Minute, func(t *testing.T, s *testutil.RepoState, ctx context.Context) {
    49→		if !s.IsExternalAgent() {
    50→			t.Skip("skipping external agent test for non-external agent")
    51→		}
    52→
    53→		_, err := s.RunPrompt(t, ctx,
    54→			"create a file called src/alpha.txt")
    55→		if err != nil {
    56→			t.Fatalf("first prompt failed: %v", err)
    57→		}
    58→
    59→		_, err = s.RunPrompt(t, ctx,
    60→			"create a file called src/beta.txt")
    61→		if err != nil {
    62→			t.Fatalf("second prompt failed: %v", err)
    63→		}
    64→
    65→		testutil.AssertFileExists(t, s.Dir, "src/alpha.txt")
    66→		testutil.AssertFileExists(t, s.Dir, "src/beta.txt")
    67→
    68→		s.Git(t, "add", ".")
    69→		s.Git(t, "commit", "-m", "Add alpha and beta via external agent")
    70→
    71→		testutil.AssertNewCommits(t, s, 1)
    72→		testutil.WaitForCheckpoint(t, s, 15*time.Second)
    73→		testutil.AssertCheckpointAdvanced(t, s)
    74→
    75→		cpID := testutil.AssertHasCheckpointTrailer(t, s.Dir, "HEAD")
    76→		testutil.AssertCheckpointExists(t, s.Dir, cpID)
    77→		testutil.AssertCheckpointMetadataComplete(t, s.Dir, cpID)
    78→		testutil.AssertNoShadowBranches(t, s.Dir)
    79→
    80→		// Both files should appear in files_touched
    81→		testutil.AssertCheckpointFilesTouchedContains(t, s.Dir, cpID, "src/alpha.txt")
    82→		testutil.AssertCheckpointFilesTouchedContains(t, s.Dir, cpID, "src/beta.txt")
    83→	})
    84→}
    85→
    86→// TestExternalAgentDeepCheckpointValidation: verifies transcript content,
    87→// content hash, and prompt text are correctly captured through the external
    88→// agent protocol.
    89→func TestExternalAgentDeepCheckpointValidation(t *testing.T) {
    90→	testutil.ForEachAgent(t, 2*time.Minute, func(t *testing.T, s *testutil.RepoState, ctx context.Context) {
    91→		_, err := s.RunPrompt(t, ctx,
    92→			"create a file called notes/deep.md")
    93→		if err != nil {
    94→			t.Fatalf("agent failed: %v", err)
    95→		}
    96→
    97→		testutil.AssertFileExists(t, s.Dir, "notes/deep.md")
    98→
    99→		s.Git(t, "add", ".")
   100→		s.Git(t, "commit", "-m", "Deep checkpoint validation test")
   101→
   102→		testutil.WaitForCheckpoint(t, s, 15*time.Second)
   103→
   104→		cpID := testutil.AssertHasCheckpointTrailer(t, s.Dir, "HEAD")
   105→
   106→		testutil.ValidateCheckpointDeep(t, s.Dir, testutil.DeepCheckpointValidation{
   107→			CheckpointID:    cpID,
   108→			Strategy:        "manual-commit",
   109→			FilesTouched:    []string{"notes/deep.md"},
   110→			ExpectedPrompts: []string{"create a file called notes/deep.md"},
   111→		})
   112→	})
   113→}
   114→
   115→// TestExternalAgentSessionMetadata: verifies that checkpoint session metadata
   116→// correctly identifies the external agent type.
   117→func TestExternalAgentSessionMetadata(t *testing.T) {
   118→	testutil.ForEachAgent(t, 2*time.Minute, func(t *testing.T, s *testutil.RepoState, ctx context.Context) {
   119→		_, err := s.RunPrompt(t, ctx,
   120→			"create a file called meta/test.md")
   121→		if err != nil {
   122→			t.Fatalf("agent failed: %v", err)
   123→		}
   124→
   125→		s.Git(t, "add", ".")
   126→		s.Git(t, "commit", "-m", "Metadata test commit")
   127→
   128→		testutil.WaitForCheckpoint(t, s, 15*time.Second)
   129→
   130→		cpID := testutil.AssertHasCheckpointTrailer(t, s.Dir, "HEAD")
   131→
   132→		// Verify session metadata has a non-empty agent field
   133→		sm := testutil.ReadSessionMetadata(t, s.Dir, cpID, 0)
   134→		assert.NotEmpty(t, sm.Agent, "session metadata should have agent field set")
   135→		assert.NotEmpty(t, sm.SessionID, "session metadata should have session_id")
   136→	})
   137→}
   138→

1→package testutil
     2→
     3→import (
     4→	"context"
     5→	"encoding/json"
     6→	"errors"
     7→	"fmt"
     8→	"os"
     9→	"os/exec"
    10→	"path/filepath"
    11→	"strings"
    12→	"testing"
    13→	"time"
    14→
    15→	"github.com/entireio/cli/e2e/agents"
    16→	"github.com/entireio/cli/e2e/entire"
    17→)
    18→
    19→const droidRepoSettingsPath = ".factory/settings.json"
    20→
    21→// RepoState holds the working state for a single test's cloned repository.
    22→type RepoState struct {
    23→	Agent            agents.Agent
    24→	Dir              string
    25→	ArtifactDir      string
    26→	HeadBefore       string
    27→	CheckpointBefore string
    28→	ConsoleLog       *os.File
    29→	session          agents.Session // interactive session, if started via StartSession
    30→	skipArtifacts    bool           // suppresses artifact capture on scenario restart
    31→}
    32→
    33→// SetupRepo creates a fresh git repository in a temporary directory, seeds it
    34→// with an initial commit, and runs `entire enable` for the given agent.
    35→// Artifact capture is registered as a cleanup function.
    36→//
    37→// When E2E_KEEP_REPOS is set, the temporary directory is not cleaned up
    38→// so it can be inspected after the test. A symlink in the artifact dir
    39→// points to the preserved repo.
    40→func SetupRepo(t *testing.T, agent agents.Agent) *RepoState {
    41→	t.Helper()
    42→
    43→	keepRepos := os.Getenv("E2E_KEEP_REPOS") != ""
    44→
    45→	// Always use os.MkdirTemp instead of t.TempDir(). Go's t.TempDir()
    46→	// creates nested subdirectories (TestName.../001/) whose structure
    47→	// confuses some agents' (e.g. opencode) working-directory resolution.
    48→	dir, err := os.MkdirTemp("", "e2e-repo-*")
    49→	if err != nil {
    50→		t.Fatalf("create temp dir: %v", err)
    51→	}
    52→	if keepRepos {
    53→		t.Logf("E2E_KEEP_REPOS: repo will be preserved at %s", dir)
    54→	} else {
    55→		t.Cleanup(func() { os.RemoveAll(dir) })
    56→	}
    57→
    58→	// Resolve symlinks (macOS: /var -> /private/var) so paths match
    59→	// what agent CLIs see when they resolve their own CWD.
    60→	if resolved, err := filepath.EvalSymlinks(dir); err == nil {
    61→		dir = resolved
    62→	}
    63→
    64→	Git(t, dir, "init")
    65→	Git(t, dir, "config", "user.name", "E2E Test")
    66→	Git(t, dir, "config", "user.email", "e2e@test.local")
    67→	Git(t, dir, "commit", "--allow-empty", "-m", "initial commit")
    68→
    69→	// External agents need external_agents enabled in settings before enable,
    70→	// so the CLI can discover the agent binary via PATH during DiscoverAndRegister.
    71→	if ea, ok := agent.(agents.ExternalAgent); ok && ea.IsExternalAgent() {
    72→		entireDir := filepath.Join(dir, ".entire")
    73→		if err := os.MkdirAll(entireDir, 0o755); err != nil {
    74→			t.Fatalf("create .entire for external agent: %v", err)
    75→		}
    76→		if err := os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(entireDir, "settings.json"),
    77→			[]byte("{\"external_agents\": true}\n"), 0o644); err != nil {
    78→			t.Fatalf("write external_agents setting: %v", err)
    79→		}
    80→	}
    81→
    82→	entire.Enable(t, dir, agent.EntireAgent())
    83→	if agent.Name() == "factoryai-droid" {
    84→		if err := configureDroidRepoSettings(dir); err != nil {
    85→			t.Fatalf("configure droid repo settings: %v", err)
    86→		}
    87→	}
    88→	PatchSettings(t, dir, map[string]any{"log_level": "debug"})
    89→
    90→	// Copilot CLI blocks on a "No copilot instructions found" notice in fresh
    91→	// repos that lack .github/copilot-instructions.md, preventing the interactive
    92→	// prompt from appearing.
    93→	if agent.Name() == "copilot-cli" {
    94→		ghDir := filepath.Join(dir, ".github")
    95→		if err := os.MkdirAll(ghDir, 0o755); err != nil {
    96→			t.Fatalf("create .github dir: %v", err)
    97→		}
    98→		if err := os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(ghDir, "copilot-instructions.md"), []byte("# E2E Test\n"), 0o644); err != nil {
    99→			t.Fatalf("write copilot-instructions.md: %v", err)
   100→		}

1→//go:build e2e
     2→
     3→package tests
     4→
     5→import (
     6→	"fmt"
     7→	"os"
     8→	"os/exec"
     9→	"path/filepath"
    10→	"runtime"
    11→	"testing"
    12→
    13→	"github.com/entireio/cli/e2e/agents"
    14→	"github.com/entireio/cli/e2e/entire"
    15→	"github.com/entireio/cli/e2e/testutil"
    16→)
    17→
    18→func TestMain(m *testing.M) {
    19→	runDir := os.Getenv("E2E_ARTIFACT_DIR")
    20→	if runDir == "" {
    21→		_, file, _, _ := runtime.Caller(0)
    22→		testutil.ArtifactRoot = filepath.Join(filepath.Dir(file), "..", "artifacts")
    23→		runDir = testutil.ArtifactRunDir()
    24→	}
    25→	_ = os.MkdirAll(runDir, 0o755)
    26→	testutil.SetRunDir(runDir)
    27→
    28→	// Resolve the entire binary (set by mise run build via E2E_ENTIRE_BIN).
    29→	entireBin := entire.BinPath()
    30→
    31→	// Prepend the binary's directory to PATH so that git hooks and agent
    32→	// hooks (which call bare "entire") resolve to the same binary the test
    33→	// harness uses, not a system-installed one.
    34→	os.Setenv("PATH", filepath.Dir(entireBin)+string(os.PathListSeparator)+os.Getenv("PATH"))
    35→
    36→	// Preflight: verify required dependencies before running any tests.
    37→	var missing []string
    38→	for _, bin := range []string{"git", "tmux"} {
    39→		if _, err := exec.LookPath(bin); err != nil {
    40→			missing = append(missing, bin)
    41→		}
    42→	}
    43→	for _, a := range agents.All() {
    44→		if _, err := exec.LookPath(a.Binary()); err != nil {
    45→			missing = append(missing, fmt.Sprintf("%s (%s)", a.Binary(), a.Name()))
    46→		}
    47→	}
    48→	if len(missing) > 0 {
    49→		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "preflight: missing required binaries: %v\n", missing)
    50→		os.Exit(1)
    51→	}
    52→
    53→	version := "unknown"
    54→	if out, err := exec.Command(entireBin, "version").Output(); err == nil {
    55→		version = string(out)
    56→	}
    57→
    58→	// Write preflight info to artifact dir only — gotestsum swallows both
    59→	// stdout and stderr, so the test-e2e script cats this file at the end.
    60→	preflight := fmt.Sprintf("entire binary:  %s\nentire version: %s\n",
    61→		entireBin, version)
    62→	_ = os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(runDir, "entire-version.txt"), []byte(preflight), 0o644)
    63→
    64→	// Don't look at user's Git config, ignore everything except the project-local Git settings.
    65→	// This avoids oddball configs in ~/.gitconfig messing with our E2E tests.
    66→	os.Setenv("GIT_CONFIG_GLOBAL", "/dev/null")
    67→
    68→	os.Exit(m.Run())
    69→}
    70→

1→package agents
     2→
     3→import (
     4→	"context"
     5→	"os"
     6→	"strconv"
     7→	"strings"
     8→	"time"
     9→)
    10→
    11→type Output struct {
    12→	Command  string
    13→	Stdout   string
    14→	Stderr   string
    15→	ExitCode int
    16→}
    17→
    18→type Option func(*runConfig)
    19→type runConfig struct {
    20→	Model          string
    21→	PermissionMode string
    22→	PromptTimeout  time.Duration
    23→}
    24→
    25→func WithModel(model string) Option {
    26→	return func(c *runConfig) { c.Model = model }
    27→}
    28→
    29→func WithPermissionMode(mode string) Option {
    30→	return func(c *runConfig) { c.PermissionMode = mode }
    31→}
    32→
    33→func WithPromptTimeout(d time.Duration) Option {
    34→	return func(c *runConfig) { c.PromptTimeout = d }
    35→}
    36→
    37→type Agent interface {
    38→	Name() string
    39→	// Binary returns the CLI binary name (e.g. "claude", "gemini").
    40→	Binary() string
    41→	EntireAgent() string
    42→	PromptPattern() string
    43→	// TimeoutMultiplier returns a factor applied to per-test timeouts.
    44→	// Slower agents (e.g. Gemini) return values > 1.
    45→	TimeoutMultiplier() float64
    46→	RunPrompt(ctx context.Context, dir string, prompt string, opts ...Option) (Output, error)
    47→	StartSession(ctx context.Context, dir string) (Session, error)
    48→	// Bootstrap performs one-time CI setup (auth config, warmup, etc.).
    49→	// Called before any tests run. Implementations should be idempotent.
    50→	Bootstrap() error
    51→	// IsTransientError returns true if the error from RunPrompt looks like
    52→	// a transient API failure (e.g. 500, rate limit, network error) that
    53→	// is worth retrying.
    54→	IsTransientError(out Output, err error) bool
    55→}
    56→
    57→type Session interface {
    58→	Send(input string) error
    59→	WaitFor(pattern string, timeout time.Duration) (string, error)
    60→	Capture() string
    61→	Close() error
    62→}
    63→
    64→// ExternalAgent is an optional interface for agents discovered via the
    65→// external agent protocol (entire-agent-* binaries). SetupRepo uses this
    66→// to pre-configure external_agents in settings before running `entire enable`.
    67→type ExternalAgent interface {
    68→	IsExternalAgent() bool
    69→}
    70→
    71→var registry []Agent
    72→var gates = map[string]chan struct{}{}
    73→
    74→func Register(a Agent) {
    75→	registry = append(registry, a)
    76→}
    77→
    78→// RegisterGate sets a concurrency limit for an agent's tests.
    79→// Tests call AcquireSlot/ReleaseSlot to respect this limit.
    80→// The limit can be overridden via E2E_CONCURRENT_TEST_LIMIT.

1→//go:build e2e

     3→package e2e
     4→
     5→import (
     6→	"encoding/json"
     7→	"testing"
     8→)
     9→
    10→// HookInput builds stdin payloads for hook-related subcommands.
    11→type HookInput struct {
    12→	HookType   string                 `json:"hook_type,omitempty"`
    13→	SessionID  string                 `json:"session_id,omitempty"`
    14→	SessionRef string                 `json:"session_ref,omitempty"`
    15→	Timestamp  string                 `json:"timestamp,omitempty"`
    16→	UserPrompt string                 `json:"user_prompt,omitempty"`
    17→	ToolName   string                 `json:"tool_name,omitempty"`
    18→	ToolUseID  string                 `json:"tool_use_id,omitempty"`
    19→	ToolInput  json.RawMessage        `json:"tool_input,omitempty"`
    20→	RawData    map[string]interface{} `json:"raw_data,omitempty"`
    21→}
    22→
    23→// JSON returns the JSON-encoded string for use as stdin.
    24→func (h HookInput) JSON(t *testing.T) string {
    25→	t.Helper()
    26→	data, err := json.Marshal(h)

## E2E Tests in the Entire CLI Codebase

I found **two main E2E test suites** in your workspace:

### 1. **Main CLI E2E Tests** (`/Users/alisha/Projects/devenv/cli/e2e/`)

This is the comprehensive E2E test suite for the Entire CLI with **~2,133 lines of code** across multiple test files. Structure:

#### Test Files (20 tests total):
- `/Users/alisha/Projects/devenv/cli/e2e/tests/`
  - `external_agent_test.go` - Tests external agent protocol flow (discovery, hooks, transcripts, checkpoints)
  - `rewind_test.go` - Tests rewinding to earlier checkpoints
  - `single_session_test.go` - Tests single session workflows
  - `session_lifecycle_test.go` - Tests session initialization/teardown
  - `multi_session_test.go` - Tests concurrent sessions
  - `resume_test.go` - Tests resuming interrupted work
  - `checkpoint_metadata_test.go` - Tests checkpoint metadata creation/validation
  - `attribution_test.go` - Tests commit attribution
  - `interactive_test.go` - Tests interactive agent sessions
  - `existing_files_test.go` - Tests handling of pre-existing files
  - `deleted_files_test.go` - Tests file deletion tracking
  - `disable_test.go` - Tests disabling Entire
  - `edge_cases_test.go` - Edge case scenarios
  - `mid_turn_commit_test.go` - Tests commits during multi-turn conversations
  - `stash_workflows_test.go` - Tests stashing workflows
  - `split_commits_test.go` - Tests splitting commits
  - `subagent_commit_flow_test.go` - Tests subagent commit flows
  - `main_test.go` - Test setup and preflight checks

#### Agent Implementations (10 agents):
- `/Users/alisha/Projects/devenv/cli/e2e/agents/`
  - `claude.go` - Claude Code agent
  - `gemini.go` - Gemini CLI agent
  - `opencode.go` - OpenCode agent
  - `cursor_cli.go` - Cursor CLI agent
  - `droid.go` - Factory AI Droid agent
  - `copilot-cli.go` - GitHub Copilot CLI agent
  - `vogon.go` - Vogon agent
  - `roger_roger.go` - Roger Roger agent
  - `tmux.go` - TMux session management
  - `agent.go` - Base Agent interface and registry

#### Test Infrastructure:
- `/Users/alisha/Projects/devenv/cli/e2e/testutil/`
  - `repo.go` - Repository setup and lifecycle management
  - `assertions.go` - Test assertions for checkpoints/files
  - `artifacts.go` - Test artifact capture
  - `metadata.go` - Checkpoint metadata validation

- `/Users/alisha/Projects/devenv/cli/e2e/entire/`
  - `entire.go` - CLI wrapper for enable, rewind, etc.

- `/Users/alisha/Projects/devenv/cli/e2e/bootstrap/main.go`
  - CI pre-test setup (auth, warmup)

#### Key Test Patterns:
- **`testutil.ForEachAgent()`** - Runs each test against all registered agents
- **`s.RunPrompt()`** - Submits agent prompts with logging
- **`s.Git()`** - Runs git operations and logs them
- **Checkpoint validation** - Verifies checkpoint creation, metadata, files touched
- **External agent protocol** - Tests discovery, hook firing, transcript handling

#### Environment Variables:
- `E2E_AGENT` - Filter agents (`claude-code`, `gemini-cli`, etc.)
- `E2E_ENTIRE_BIN` - Pre-built binary path
- `E2E_TIMEOUT` - Per-prompt timeout (default: 2m)
- `E2E_KEEP_REPOS` - Preserve temp repos for inspection
- `E2E_ARTIFACT_DIR` - Override artifact directory

#### Artifact Capture:
- `e2e/artifacts/<timestamp>/` contains test results including:
  - `console.log` - Full operation transcript
  - `git-log.txt`, `git-tree.txt` - Git state at failure
  - `entire-logs/` - CLI internal logs
  - Per-test directories with detailed outputs

### 2. **External Agents (Kiro) E2E Tests** (`/Users/alisha/Projects/external-agents/e2e/`)

Focused E2E tests for the **Kiro external agent** implementation. Files:
- `/Users/alisha/Projects/external-agents/e2e/kiro_test.go` - **~600 test functions** covering:
  - Identity/info endpoints
  - Detection (`TestKiro_Detect_Present/Absent`)
  - Session management (get/resolve/read/write sessions)
  - Transcript operations (read, chunk, reassemble)
  - Hook parsing (spawn, prompt-submit, pre-tool-use, stop)
  - Hook installation/uninstallation
  - Transcript analysis (position, modified files, prompts, summary)
  - Idempotency checks

- Support files:
  - `testenv.go` - Test environment setup
  - `harness.go` - Test runner harness
  - `fixtures.go` - Test data builders (HookInput, KiroTranscript, etc.)
  - `setup_test.go` - Setup helpers
  - `go.mod` - Module definition

### CI/CD Integration:
- `.github/workflows/e2e.yml` - Full matrix test run on main push
- `.github/workflows/e2e-isolated.yml` - Manual dispatch for single test debugging
- Shell scripts: `test-attribution-e2e.sh`, `download-e2e-artifacts.sh`

### Key Differences:
- **CLI E2E tests**: Test Entire CLI against real agent CLIs, verify checkpoint/transcript integration
- **Kiro tests**: Unit/integration tests for external agent protocol implementation (hooks, sessions, transcripts)

All tests use the `//go:build e2e` tag to exclude them from normal builds (must be explicitly run with `-tags=e2e`).

    10→	"github.com/entireio/cli/e2e/testutil"
    11→	"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
    12→)
    13→
    14→// TestExternalAgentSingleSessionManualCommit: external agent creates a file,
    15→// user commits manually. Validates the full external agent protocol flow:
    16→// discovery via PATH, hook firing through binary subcommands, transcript
    17→// handling, and checkpoint creation.
    18→func TestExternalAgentSingleSessionManualCommit(t *testing.T) {
    19→	testutil.ForEachAgent(t, 2*time.Minute, func(t *testing.T, s *testutil.RepoState, ctx context.Context) {
    20→		_, err := s.RunPrompt(t, ctx,
    21→			"create a file called docs/hello.md")
    22→		if err != nil {
    23→			t.Fatalf("agent failed: %v", err)
    24→		}
    25→
    26→		testutil.AssertFileExists(t, s.Dir, "docs/hello.md")
    27→
    28→		s.Git(t, "add", ".")
    29→		s.Git(t, "commit", "-m", "Add hello file via external agent")
    30→
    31→		testutil.AssertNewCommits(t, s, 1)
    32→		testutil.WaitForCheckpoint(t, s, 15*time.Second)
    33→		testutil.AssertCheckpointAdvanced(t, s)
    34→
    35→		cpID := testutil.AssertHasCheckpointTrailer(t, s.Dir, "HEAD")
    36→		testutil.AssertCheckpointExists(t, s.Dir, cpID)
    37→		testutil.AssertCheckpointMetadataComplete(t, s.Dir, cpID)
    38→		testutil.AssertCheckpointHasSingleSession(t, s.Dir, cpID)
    39→		testutil.AssertCheckpointFilesTouchedContains(t, s.Dir, cpID, "docs/hello.md")
    40→		testutil.AssertNoShadowBranches(t, s.Dir)
    41→	})
    42→}
    43→
    44→// TestExternalAgentMultipleTurnsManualCommit: external agent handles two
    45→// sequential prompts, user commits once. Both prompts should be captured
    46→// in a single checkpoint.
    47→func TestExternalAgentMultipleTurnsManualCommit(t *testing.T) {
    48→	testutil.ForEachAgent(t, 2*time.Minute, func(t *testing.T, s *testutil.RepoState, ctx context.Context) {
    49→		if !s.IsExternalAgent() {
    50→			t.Skip("skipping external agent test for non-external agent")
    51→		}
    52→
    53→		_, err := s.RunPrompt(t, ctx,
    54→			"create a file called src/alpha.txt")
    55→		if err != nil {
    56→			t.Fatalf("first prompt failed: %v", err)
    57→		}
    58→
    59→		_, err = s.RunPrompt(t, ctx,

    61→		if err != nil {

    63→		}
    64→
    65→		testutil.AssertFileExists(t, s.Dir, "src/alpha.txt")
    66→		testutil.AssertFileExists(t, s.Dir, "src/beta.txt")
    67→
    68→		s.Git(t, "add", ".")

    70→
    71→		testutil.AssertNewCommits(t, s, 1)
    72→		testutil.WaitForCheckpoint(t, s, 15*time.Second)
    73→		testutil.AssertCheckpointAdvanced(t, s)
    74→
    75→		cpID := testutil.AssertHasCheckpointTrailer(t, s.Dir, "HEAD")
    76→		testutil.AssertCheckpointExists(t, s.Dir, cpID)
    77→		testutil.AssertCheckpointMetadataComplete(t, s.Dir, cpID)
    78→		testutil.AssertNoShadowBranches(t, s.Dir)
    79→
    80→		// Both files should appear in files_touched

    82→		testutil.AssertCheckpointFilesTouchedContains(t, s.Dir, cpID, "src/beta.txt")

   115→// TestExternalAgentSessionMetadata: verifies that checkpoint session metadata

1→//go:build e2e
     2→
     3→package tests
     4→
     5→import (
     6→	"context"
     7→	"fmt"
     8→	"os"
     9→	"path/filepath"
    10→	"testing"
    11→	"time"
    12→
    13→	"github.com/entireio/cli/e2e/entire"
    14→	"github.com/entireio/cli/e2e/testutil"
    15→	"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
    16→	"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
    17→)
    18→
    19→// TestRewindPreCommit: agent creates two files in sequence (no user commit),
    20→// then rewinds to the point after the first file was created.
    21→func TestRewindPreCommit(t *testing.T) {
    22→	testutil.ForEachAgent(t, 3*time.Minute, func(t *testing.T, s *testutil.RepoState, ctx context.Context) {
    23→		// Agent creates file A.
    24→		_, err := s.RunPrompt(t, ctx,
    25→			"create a markdown file at docs/red.md with a paragraph about the colour red. Do not ask for confirmation, just make the change.")
    26→		if err != nil {
    27→			t.Fatalf("agent prompt 1 failed: %v", err)
    28→		}
    29→		testutil.AssertFileExists(t, s.Dir, "docs/red.md")
    30→
    31→		pointsAfterA := entire.RewindList(t, s.Dir)
    32→		require.NotEmpty(t, pointsAfterA, "expected at least 1 rewind point after file A")
    33→
    34→		// Agent creates file B.
    35→		_, err = s.RunPrompt(t, ctx,
    36→			"create a markdown file at docs/blue.md with a paragraph about the colour blue. Do not ask for confirmation, just make the change.")
    37→		if err != nil {
    38→			t.Fatalf("agent prompt 2 failed: %v", err)
    39→		}
    40→		testutil.AssertFileExists(t, s.Dir, "docs/blue.md")
    41→
    42→		pointsAfterB := entire.RewindList(t, s.Dir)
    43→		require.Greater(t, len(pointsAfterB), len(pointsAfterA),
    44→			"expected more rewind points after file B")
    45→
    46→		// Rewind to the first rewind point (after file A was created).
    47→		rewindID := pointsAfterA[0].ID
    48→		err = entire.Rewind(t, s.Dir, rewindID)
    49→		require.NoError(t, err, "rewind to %s should succeed", rewindID)
    50→
    51→		// File A should still exist, file B should be gone.
    52→		testutil.AssertFileExists(t, s.Dir, "docs/red.md")
    53→		_, statErr := os.Stat(filepath.Join(s.Dir, "docs", "blue.md"))
    54→		assert.True(t, os.IsNotExist(statErr),
    55→			"docs/blue.md should not exist after rewind, stat error: %v", statErr)
    56→	})
    57→}
    58→
    59→// TestRewindAfterCommit: pre-commit shadow branch rewind points should become
    60→// invalid after a user commit. Rewinding to an old shadow branch ID should fail.
    61→func TestRewindAfterCommit(t *testing.T) {
    62→	testutil.ForEachAgent(t, 3*time.Minute, func(t *testing.T, s *testutil.RepoState, ctx context.Context) {
    63→		// Agent creates a file.
    64→		_, err := s.RunPrompt(t, ctx,
    65→			"create a markdown file at docs/red.md with a paragraph about the colour red. Do not commit the file. Do not ask for confirmation, just make the change.")
    66→		if err != nil {
    67→			t.Fatalf("agent failed: %v", err)
    68→		}
    69→		testutil.AssertFileExists(t, s.Dir, "docs/red.md")
    70→
    71→		// Get pre-commit rewind points (shadow branch).
    72→		pointsBefore := entire.RewindList(t, s.Dir)
    73→		require.NotEmpty(t, pointsBefore, "expected rewind points before commit")
    74→
    75→		// Find a non-logs-only point (shadow branch ID).
    76→		var shadowPoint *entire.RewindPoint
    77→		for i := range pointsBefore {
    78→			if !pointsBefore[i].IsLogsOnly {
    79→				shadowPoint = &pointsBefore[i]
    80→				break
    81→			}
    82→		}
    83→		require.NotNil(t, shadowPoint, "expected at least one shadow branch rewind point")
    84→		oldID := shadowPoint.ID
    85→
    86→		// User commits the file.
    87→		s.Git(t, "add", ".")
    88→		s.Git(t, "commit", "-m", "Add red.md")
    89→
    90→		testutil.WaitForCheckpoint(t, s, 15*time.Second)
    91→
    92→		// Get rewind points after commit — old shadow IDs should be gone.
    93→		pointsAfter := entire.RewindList(t, s.Dir)
    94→
    95→		// Verify the old shadow branch ID is no longer listed.
    96→		found := false
    97→		for _, p := range pointsAfter {
    98→			if p.ID == oldID && !p.IsLogsOnly {
    99→				found = true
   100→				break
   101→			}
   102→		}
   103→		assert.False(t, found, "old shadow branch rewind point %s should no longer be listed", oldID)
   104→
   105→		// Attempting to rewind to the old shadow branch ID should fail.
   106→		err = entire.Rewind(t, s.Dir, oldID)
   107→		assert.Error(t, err, "rewind to old shadow branch ID should fail after commit")
   108→
   109→		// Working directory should be unchanged — file still committed.
   110→		testutil.AssertFileExists(t, s.Dir, "docs/red.md")
   111→		testutil.AssertNoShadowBranches(t, s.Dir)
   112→	})
   113→}
   114→
   115→// TestRewindMultipleFiles: agent creates files across two prompts, rewind
   116→// drops only the second prompt's changes.
   117→func TestRewindMultipleFiles(t *testing.T) {
   118→	testutil.ForEachAgent(t, 3*time.Minute, func(t *testing.T, s *testutil.RepoState, ctx context.Context) {
   119→		// First prompt — create file A. Use non-colour filename to avoid
   120→		// Gemini "helpfully" creating the entire rainbow.
   121→		_, err := s.RunPrompt(t, ctx,
   122→			"create a markdown file at docs/readme.md with a paragraph about this project. Do not create any other files. Do not ask for confirmation, just make the change.")
   123→		if err != nil {
   124→			t.Fatalf("agent prompt 1 failed: %v", err)
   125→		}
   126→		testutil.AssertFileExists(t, s.Dir, "docs/readme.md")
   127→
   128→		// Capture rewind points after first prompt.
   129→		pointsAfterA := entire.RewindList(t, s.Dir)
   130→		require.NotEmpty(t, pointsAfterA, "expected rewind points after first prompt")
   131→
   132→		// Second prompt — create file B (unrelated topic).
   133→		_, err = s.RunPrompt(t, ctx,
   134→			"create a markdown file at docs/changelog.md with a paragraph about recent changes. Do not create any other files. Do not ask for confirmation, just make the change.")
   135→		if err != nil {
   136→			t.Fatalf("agent prompt 2 failed: %v", err)
   137→		}
   138→		testutil.AssertFileExists(t, s.Dir, "docs/changelog.md")
   139→
   140→		// Rewind to a point from after the first prompt.
   141→		rewindID := pointsAfterA[0].ID
   142→		err = entire.Rewind(t, s.Dir, rewindID)
   143→		require.NoError(t, err, "rewind to %s should succeed", rewindID)
   144→
   145→		// File A should remain, file B should be gone.
   146→		testutil.AssertFileExists(t, s.Dir, "docs/readme.md")
   147→		_, statErr := os.Stat(filepath.Join(s.Dir, "docs", "changelog.md"))
   148→		assert.True(t, os.IsNotExist(statErr),
   149→			"docs/changelog.md should not exist after rewind, stat error: %v", statErr)
   150→	})
   151→}
   152→
   153→// TestRewindSquashMergeMultipleCheckpoints: when a squash merge commit contains
   154→// multiple Entire-Checkpoint trailers, `entire rewind --list` should show a
   155→// single logs-only point using the latest checkpoint. Logs-only restore should
   156→// succeed and return the most recent session's transcript.
   157→func TestRewindSquashMergeMultipleCheckpoints(t *testing.T) {
   158→	testutil.ForEachAgent(t, 5*time.Minute, func(t *testing.T, s *testutil.RepoState, ctx context.Context) {
   159→		mainBranch := testutil.GitOutput(t, s.Dir, "branch", "--show-current")
   160→
   161→		// Commit files from `entire enable` so main has a clean working tree.
   162→		s.Git(t, "add", ".")
   163→		s.Git(t, "commit", "-m", "Enable entire")
   164→
   165→		// Create feature branch with two agent-assisted commits.
   166→		s.Git(t, "checkout", "-b", "feature")
   167→
   168→		_, err := s.RunPrompt(t, ctx,
   169→			"create a file at docs/red.md with a paragraph about the colour red. Do not ask for confirmation, just make the change.")
   170→		if err != nil {
   171→			t.Fatalf("prompt 1 failed: %v", err)
   172→		}
   173→
   174→		s.Git(t, "add", ".")
   175→		s.Git(t, "commit", "-m", "Add red doc")
   176→		testutil.WaitForCheckpoint(t, s, 15*time.Second)
   177→		cp1Ref := testutil.GitOutput(t, s.Dir, "rev-parse", "entire/checkpoints/v1")
   178→
   179→		_, err = s.RunPrompt(t, ctx,
   180→			"create a file at docs/blue.md with a paragraph about the colour blue. Do not ask for confirmation, just make the change.")
   181→		if err != nil {
   182→			t.Fatalf("prompt 2 failed: %v", err)
   183→		}
   184→
   185→		s.Git(t, "add", ".")
   186→		s.Git(t, "commit", "-m", "Add blue doc")
   187→		testutil.WaitForCheckpointAdvanceFrom(t, s.Dir, cp1Ref, 15*time.Second)
   188→
   189→		// Record checkpoint IDs from both feature branch commits.
   190→		cpID1 := testutil.GetCheckpointTrailer(t, s.Dir, "HEAD~1")
   191→		cpID2 := testutil.GetCheckpointTrailer(t, s.Dir, "HEAD")
   192→		require.NotEmpty(t, cpID1, "first commit should have checkpoint trailer")
   193→		require.NotEmpty(t, cpID2, "second commit should have checkpoint trailer")
   194→		require.NotEqual(t, cpID1, cpID2, "checkpoint IDs should be distinct")
   195→
   196→		// Squash merge onto main with both checkpoint trailers in the message.
   197→		s.Git(t, "checkout", mainBranch)
   198→		s.Git(t, "merge", "--squash", "feature")
   199→		squashMsg := fmt.Sprintf(
   200→			"Squash merge feature (#1)\n\n* Add red doc\n\nEntire-Checkpoint: %s\n\n* Add blue doc\n\nEntire-Checkpoint: %s",
   201→			cpID1, cpID2,
   202→		)
   203→		s.Git(t, "commit", "-m", squashMsg)
   204→		squashHash := testutil.GitOutput(t, s.Dir, "rev-parse", "HEAD")
   205→
   206→		// Rewind list should show exactly one logs-only point for the squash commit,
   207→		// using the latest checkpoint (cpID2).
   208→		points := entire.RewindList(t, s.Dir)
   209→
   210→		var squashPoints []entire.RewindPoint
   211→		for _, p := range points {
   212→			if p.ID == squashHash && p.IsLogsOnly {
   213→				squashPoints = append(squashPoints, p)
   214→			}
   215→		}
   216→		require.Len(t, squashPoints, 1,
   217→			"expected exactly 1 logs-only rewind point for squash commit")
   218→		assert.Equal(t, cpID2, squashPoints[0].CondensationID,
   219→			"squash merge rewind point should use the latest checkpoint ID")
   220→
   221→		// Logs-only restore should succeed.
   222→		err = entire.RewindLogsOnly(t, s.Dir, squashHash)
   223→		require.NoError(t, err, "logs-only rewind of squash merge commit should succeed")
   224→	})
   225→}
   226→

1→#!/bin/sh
     2→#MISE description="Run E2E tests with roger-roger external agent (no API calls)"
     3→#USAGE arg "[filter]" help="Test name filter (regex)" default=""
     4→
     5→set -eu
     6→
     7→export E2E_AGENT="roger-roger"
     8→
     9→# Build entire CLI
    10→mise run build
    11→export E2E_ENTIRE_BIN="$PWD/entire"
    12→
    13→# Ensure roger-roger binaries are available on PATH before running E2E tests.
    14→# They are typically installed via mise (see mise.toml [tools]), but we fail fast
    15→# here if they are missing so the suite cannot silently "succeed" without
    16→# exercising the external agent.
    17→if ! command -v roger-roger >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    18→  echo "error: 'roger-roger' binary not found on PATH. Install it via 'mise install' or ensure it is available before running this task." >&2
    19→  exit 1
    20→fi
    21→
    22→if ! command -v entire-agent-roger-roger >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    23→  echo "error: 'entire-agent-roger-roger' binary not found on PATH. Install it via 'mise install' or ensure it is available before running this task." >&2
    24→  exit 1
    25→fi
    26→
    27→if [ -z "${E2E_ARTIFACT_DIR:-}" ]; then
    28→  E2E_ARTIFACT_DIR="$PWD/e2e/artifacts/roger-roger-$(date +%Y-%m-%dT%H-%M-%S)"
    29→fi
    30→export E2E_ARTIFACT_DIR
    31→mkdir -p "$E2E_ARTIFACT_DIR"
    32→echo "artifacts: $E2E_ARTIFACT_DIR"
    33→
    34→filter="${usage_filter:-}"
    35→
    36→gotestsum --format dots --jsonfile "$E2E_ARTIFACT_DIR/test-events.json" -- \
    37→  -tags=e2e -count=1 -timeout=10m \
    38→  ${filter:+-run "$filter"} \
    39→  ./e2e/tests/... || rc=$?
    40→
    41→go run ./e2e/cmd/testreport -color -o "$E2E_ARTIFACT_DIR/report.txt" "$E2E_ARTIFACT_DIR/test-events.json"
    42→echo ""
    43→cat "$E2E_ARTIFACT_DIR/entire-version.txt" 2>/dev/null
    44→echo "artifacts: $E2E_ARTIFACT_DIR"
    45→exit "${rc:-0}"
    46→

Now I have all the information I need. Let me create a comprehensive summary:

## Roger-Roger Agent Source Code Analysis

I've located and read the complete roger-roger agent source code. Here's what I found:

### Location
The source code is in the go modules cache at:
`/Users/alisha/go/pkg/mod/github.com/entireio/roger-roger@v0.0.0-20260316120302-bddb9fde1843/`

### Overview
Roger-roger is a minimal deterministic test agent with two binaries:
1. **roger-roger** - Interactive REPL that reads prompts from stdin
2. **entire-agent-roger-roger** - External agent protocol handler for the Entire CLI

Both binaries are built from a single Go module (no external dependencies except `google/uuid`).

---

## 1. REPL Binary: `roger-roger` (cmd/roger-roger/main.go)

### Prompt Interpretation
The REPL uses a regex pattern to parse prompts:
```go
var createFileRe = regexp.MustCompile(`(?i)create\s+(?:a\s+)?(?:\w+\s+)*?file\s+(?:at\s+|called\s+)?([^\s,]+\.\w+)`)
```

**Supported actions:**
- **"create a file called X"** → Creates file with header `# X\n`
- **Anything else** → Responds with "roger roger"

Example:
```
> create a file called docs/hello.md
Created docs/hello.md.
> what's up
roger roger
```

### File Creation Logic
```go
func createFile(dir, path, content string) {
    abs := filepath.Join(dir, path)
    if err := os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(abs), 0o755); err != nil { ... }
    if err := os.WriteFile(abs, []byte(content), 0o644); err != nil { ... }
}
```

Creates directories as needed (755 perms) and writes files with 644 perms.

### Lifecycle & Hook Firing

The REPL follows this lifecycle:

1. **Session Start**
   - Generates UUID as `sessionID`
   - Creates transcript at `~/.roger-roger/sessions/{sessionID}.jsonl`
   - Fires `session-start` hook with payload:
     ```json
     {
       "session_id": "{uuid}",
       "transcript_path": "~/.roger-roger/sessions/{uuid}.jsonl"
     }
     ```

2. **Read Prompt Loop**
   - Prints `> ` prompt
   - Reads line from stdin
   - Empty line, "exit", or "quit" → break loop

3. **Per Turn (runTurn)**
   - Fires `user-prompt-submit` hook:
     ```json
     {
       "session_id": "{sessionID}",
       "transcript_path": "{transcriptPath}",
       "prompt": "{user_prompt}"
     }
     ```
   - Appends user message to transcript
   - Calls `handlePrompt()` to process
   - Appends assistant response to transcript
   - Prints response to stdout
   - Fires `stop` hook:
     ```json
     {
       "session_id": "{sessionID}",
       "transcript_path": "{transcriptPath}"
     }
     ```

4. **Session End**
   - Fires `session-end` hook with session_id and transcript_path

### Hook Firing Mechanism
```go
func fireHook(dir, hookName string, payload any) {
    data, err := json.Marshal(payload)
    cmd := exec.Command("entire", "hooks", "roger-roger", hookName)
    cmd.Dir = dir
    cmd.Stdin = bytes.NewReader(data)
    cmd.Env = append(os.Environ(), "ENTIRE_TEST_TTY=0")
    cmd.CombinedOutput()
}
```

Calls `entire hooks roger-roger {hookName}` with JSON payload on stdin, ignoring output.

### Transcript Format
Uses standard JSONL format (Claude Code / Cursor):
```json
{"type":"user","uuid":"...", "message":{"content":"create a file..."}}
{"type":"assistant","uuid":"...", "message":{"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Created ..."}]}}
```

---

## 2. Protocol Handler: `entire-agent-roger-roger` (cmd/entire-agent-roger-roger/main.go)

### Subcommands (External Agent Protocol)

| Subcommand | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `info` | Declares agent capabilities |
| `detect` | Returns `{"present": false}` |
| `get-session-id` | Extracts session ID from hook input |
| `get-session-dir` | Returns `~/.roger-roger/sessions` |
| `resolve-session-file` | Maps session ID → file path |
| `read-session` | Reads session metadata |
| `write-session` | Writes session to disk |
| `read-transcript` | Reads JSONL file |
| `chunk-transcript` | Splits JSONL into chunks |
| `reassemble-transcript` | Concatenates chunks |
| `format-resume-command` | Returns `roger-roger --session-id {id}` |
| `parse-hook` | Converts hook JSON to event JSON |
| `install-hooks` / `uninstall-hooks` / `are-hooks-installed` | Hook lifecycle |
| `write-hook-response` | Prints message to stdout |
| `get-transcript-position` | Returns line count |
| `extract-modified-files` | Parses responses matching `^Created (.+)\.$` |
| `extract-prompts` | Extracts user messages |
| `extract-summary` | Summarizes (truncates first prompt to 100 chars) |

### Info Response
```json
{
  "protocol_version": 1,
  "name": "roger-roger",
  "type": "Roger Roger Agent",
  "description": "Roger Roger - just a test agent",
  "protected_dirs": [".roger-roger"],
  "hook_names": ["session-start", "session-end", "stop", "user-prompt-submit"],
  "capabilities": {
    "hooks": true,
    "hooks_response_writer": true,
    "transcript_analyzer": true
  }
}
```

### Hook Parsing (cmdParseHook)
Converts hook JSON:
```json
{"session_id":"...", "transcript_path":"...", "prompt":"..."}
```

Into event JSON:
```json
{
  "type": 1,  // 1=session-start, 2=turn-start, 3=turn-end, 5=session-end
  "session_id": "...",
  "session_ref": "...",  // Maps to transcript_path
  "prompt": "...",
  "timestamp": "2026-03-18T..."
}
```

### File Extraction
```go
var createdFileRe = regexp.MustCompile(`^Created ([^\s]+)\.$`)
```

Scans assistant messages in transcript starting from offset, collects unique filenames.

---

## 3. Full Lifecycle Example

### Command: `entire enable --agent roger-roger`
1. CLI discovers `entire-agent-roger-roger` via `$PATH`
2. Calls `entire-agent-roger-roger info` → learns capabilities, hook names
3. Stores roger-roger as enabled agent

### User Prompt Interaction
1. CLI calls `entire-agent-roger-roger get-session-dir --repo-path {path}`
   → Returns `~/.roger-roger/sessions`
2. CLI spawns `roger-roger` REPL in project directory
3. REPL fires `session-start` hook → CLI captures via `parse-hook`
4. CLI sends user prompt to REPL's stdin: `"create docs/api.md\n"`
5. REPL fires `user-prompt-submit` hook (hook parsing extracts prompt)
6. REPL creates file: `docs/api.md` with content `# docs/api.md\n`
7. REPL outputs: `Created docs/api.md.`
8. REPL fires `stop` hook
9. REPL prints next `> ` prompt
10. **Checkpoint Creation:**
    - CLI calls `extract-modified-files --path {transcript}` → finds `docs/api.md`
    - CLI calls `get-transcript-position --path {transcript}` → position = line count
    - CLI creates checkpoint with modified files list

### Multi-Turn Session
Same flow repeats for each prompt, with offset advancing through transcript.

### Session End
When user sends empty line or exits:
- REPL fires `session-end` hook
- REPL exits
- CLI calls `read-session` to serialize final state

---

## Key Files

1. **REPL Source**: `/Users/alisha/go/pkg/mod/github.com/entireio/roger-roger@v0.0.0-20260316120302-bddb9fde1843/cmd/roger-roger/main.go`
2. **Protocol Handler**: `/Users/alisha/go/pkg/mod/github.com/entireio/roger-roger@v0.0.0-20260316120302-bddb9fde1843/cmd/entire-agent-roger-roger/main.go`
3. **E2E Test Integration**: `/Users/alisha/Projects/devenv/cli/e2e/agents/roger_roger.go` - Implements Agent interface, launches REPL with stdin, feeds prompts

The agent is minimal but complete: regex-based prompt handling, deterministic file creation, proper hook firing, transcript recording in standard format, and full external agent protocol implementation.

       "transcript_path": "{transcriptPath}",
       "prompt": "{user_prompt}"
     }
     ```
   - Appends user message to transcript
   - Calls `handlePrompt()` to process
   - Appends assistant response to transcript
   - Prints response to stdout
   - Fires `stop` hook:
     ```json
     {
       "session_id": "{sessionID}",
       "transcript_path": "{transcriptPath}"
     }
     ```

4. **Session End**
   - Fires `session-end` hook with session_id and transcript_path

### Hook Firing Mechanism
```go
func fireHook(dir, hookName string, payload any) {
    data, err := json.Marshal(payload)
    cmd := exec.Command("entire", "hooks", "roger-roger", hookName)
    cmd.Dir = dir
    cmd.Stdin = bytes.NewReader(data)
    cmd.Env = append(os.Environ(), "ENTIRE_TEST_TTY=0")
    cmd.CombinedOutput()
}
```

Calls `entire hooks roger-roger {hookName}` with JSON payload on stdin, ignoring output.

### Transcript Format
Uses standard JSONL format (Claude Code / Cursor):
```json
{"type":"user","uuid":"...", "message":{"content":"create a file..."}}
{"type":"assistant","uuid":"...", "message":{"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Created ..."}]}}
```

---

## 2. Protocol Handler: `entire-agent-roger-roger` (cmd/entire-agent-roger-roger/main.go)

### Subcommands (External Agent Protocol)

| Subcommand | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `info` | Declares agent capabilities |
| `detect` | Returns `{"present": false}` |
| `get-session-id` | Extracts session ID from hook input |
| `get-session-dir` | Returns `~/.roger-roger/sessions` |
| `resolve-session-file` | Maps session ID → file path |
| `read-session` | Reads session metadata |
| `write-session` | Writes session to disk |
| `read-transcript` | Reads JSONL file |
| `chunk-transcript` | Splits JSONL into chunks |
| `reassemble-transcript` | Concatenates chunks |
| `format-resume-command` | Returns `roger-roger --session-id {id}` |
| `parse-hook` | Converts hook JSON to event JSON |
| `install-hooks` / `uninstall-hooks` / `are-hooks-installed` | Hook lifecycle |
| `write-hook-response` | Prints message to stdout |
| `get-transcript-position` | Returns line count |
| `extract-modified-files` | Parses responses matching `^Created (.+)\.$` |
| `extract-prompts` | Extracts user messages |
| `extract-summary` | Summarizes (truncates first prompt to 100 chars) |

### Info Response
```json
{
  "protocol_version": 1,
  "name": "roger-roger",
  "type": "Roger Roger Agent",
  "description": "Roger Roger - just a test agent",
  "protected_dirs": [".roger-roger"],
  "hook_names": ["session-start", "session-end", "stop", "user-prompt-submit"],
  "capabilities": {
    "hooks": true,
    "hooks_response_writer": true,
    "transcript_analyzer": true
  }
}
```

### Hook Parsing (cmdParseHook)
Converts hook JSON:
```json
{"session_id":"...", "transcript_path":"...", "prompt":"..."}
```

Into event JSON:
```json
{
  "type": 1,  // 1=session-start, 2=turn-start, 3=turn-end, 5=session-end
  "session_id": "...",
  "session_ref": "...",  // Maps to transcript_path
  "prompt": "...",
  "timestamp": "2026-03-18T..."
}
```

### File Extraction
```go
var createdFileRe = regexp.MustCompile(`^Created ([^\s]+)\.$`)
```

Scans assistant messages in transcript starting from offset, collects unique filenames.

---

## 3. Full Lifecycle Example

### Command: `entire enable --agent roger-roger`
1. CLI discovers `entire-agent-roger-roger` via `$PATH`
2. Calls `entire-agent-roger-roger info` → learns capabilities, hook names
3. Stores roger-roger as enabled agent

### User Prompt Interaction
1. CLI calls `entire-agent-roger-roger get-session-dir --repo-path {path}`
   → Returns `~/.roger-roger/sessions`
2. CLI spawns `roger-roger` REPL in project directory
3. REPL fires `session-start` hook → CLI captures via `parse-hook`
4. CLI sends user prompt to REPL's stdin: `"create docs/api.md\n"`
5. REPL fires `user-prompt-submit` hook (hook parsing extracts prompt)
6. REPL creates file: `docs/api.md` with content `# docs/api.md\n`
7. REPL outputs: `Created docs/api.md.`
8. REPL fires `stop` hook
9. REPL prints next `> ` prompt
10. **Checkpoint Creation:**
    - CLI calls `extract-modified-files --path {transcript}` → finds `docs/api.md`
    - CLI calls `get-transcript-position --path {transcript}` → position = line count
    - CLI creates checkpoint with modified files list

### Multi-Turn Session
Same flow repeats for each prompt, with offset advancing through transcript.

### Session End
When user sends empty line or exits:
- REPL fires `session-end` hook
- REPL exits
- CLI calls `read-session` to serialize final state

---

## Key Files

1. **REPL Source**: `/Users/alisha/go/pkg/mod/github.com/entireio/roger-roger@v0.0.0-20260316120302-bddb9fde1843/cmd/roger-roger/main.go`
2. **Protocol Handler**: `/Users/alisha/go/pkg/mod/github.com/entireio/roger-roger@v0.0.0-20260316120302-bddb9fde1843/cmd/entire-agent-roger-roger/main.go`
3. **E2E Test Integration**: `/Users/alisha/Projects/devenv/cli/e2e/agents/roger_roger.go` - Implements Agent interface, launches REPL with stdin, feeds prompts

The agent is minimal but complete: regex-based prompt handling, deterministic file creation, proper hook firing, transcript recording in standard format, and full external agent protocol implementation.

content.content: {"body":"## Summary - Add Kiro agent integration (both CLI and IDE modes): hook installation, lifecycle event parsing, transcript analysis, and session management - Fix shared `ReadAndParseHookInput` stdin timeout bug that could drop data for any agent with slow EOF - Guard empty transcript fallback to Kiro-only, preventing silent data loss for other agents - Strategy fixes for TTY-based agents: commit linking, transcript backfill, rewind transcript clearing ## Key changes ### New agent package (`cmd/entire/cli/agent/kiro/`) - Full `Agent` interface implementation including `HookSupport`, `TranscriptAnalyzer` - Hook installation via `.kiro/settings/hooks.json` — works with both Kiro CLI and Kiro IDE - Lifecycle event parsing (session start/stop, turn start/end) from Kiro's hook format - Transcript extraction from Kiro's SQLite-backed session format ### Shared hook input reading (`cmd/entire/cli/agent/event.go`) - Introduced `ErrEmptyHookInput` sentinel error for agents to handle gracefully - Two-phase stdin read: `Peek(1)` with 500ms timeout to detect empty pipe, then `io.ReadAll` without timeout once data is confirmed — fixes a bug where the old single-phase timeout could discard valid data if EOF was slow ### Strategy fixes (`cmd/entire/cli/strategy/`) - **Condensation**: Empty transcript fallback now guarded by `AgentTypeKiro` check; other agents get an explicit error instead of silently proceeding with no data - **Hooks**: `PrepareCommitMsg` fast path extended for TTY-based agents (Kiro in tmux) via `TerminalAgent` interface — auto-links commits during ACTIVE sessions without prompting - **Git**: Backfill `TranscriptPath` in `SaveStep` for agents with deferred transcript persistence - **Rewind**: Clear `TranscriptPath` after rewind so condensation reads from the reset shadow branch, not the stale live transcript ### E2E runner (`e2e/agents/kiro.go`) - Registers Kiro with the E2E framework - Supports both prompt and interactive session modes - **Note**: Kiro E2E tests cannot currently run in CI due to upstream issues: - [kirodotdev/Kiro#5938](https://github.com/kirodotdev/Kiro/issues/5938) — CLI auth blockers - [kirodotdev/Kiro#5358](https://github.com/kirodotdev/Kiro/issues/5358) — headless mode limitations - Kiro agent has been removed from CI E2E workflows until these are resolved ## Test plan - [x] Unit tests for hooks, lifecycle, types, transcript, and agent registration (`cmd/entire/cli/agent/kiro/*_test.go`) - [x] All tests pass: `mise run test:ci` (unit + integration + canary) - [x] E2E validated manually with Kiro CLI and Kiro IDE locally - [x] Lint clean: `mise run fmt && mise run lint` 🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code) # E2E Test Harness for External Agents

## Context

The external-agents repo has only unit tests. We need a true E2E harness that builds agent binaries and exercises them via their CLI interface (subcommands with JSON stdin/stdout), the same way the Entire CLI invokes them in production. This mirrors the pattern from `devenv/cli/cmd/entire/cli/integration_test/` but adapted for standalone agent binaries.

## Design Decisions

| Decision | Rationale |
|----------|-----------|
| Top-level `e2e/` directory | Centralizes all E2E tests, shared harness code |
| Separate Go module (`e2e/go.mod`) | Avoids import-coupling with agent modules; tests only invoke compiled binaries |
| `//go:build e2e` tag | Keeps E2E tests out of `go test ./...`; requires `-tags=e2e` |
| Auto-discovery of agents in TestMain | Adding `agents/entire-agent-foo/` auto-includes it in build step |
| `ENTIRE_REPO_ROOT` env var for isolation | Already read by `protocol.RepoRoot()` (protocol.go:73-76) |
| `HOME` override to temp dir | Isolates SQLite/IDE path lookups from real user data |

## Files to Create

### 1. `e2e/go.mod`
```
module github.com/entireio/external-agents/e2e
go 1.26.0
```
No external dependencies — stdlib only.

### 2. `e2e/setup_test.go` — TestMain (binary builder)
- Auto-discover all `agents/entire-agent-*` directories
- Build each binary once into a shared temp dir via `go build`
- Store paths in package-level `agentBinaries` map
- Clean up temp dir after all tests complete

### 3. `e2e/harness.go` — AgentRunner (generic binary invoker)
Core struct with methods:
- `Run(stdin, subcommand, args...) CommandResult` — raw execution
- `RunJSON(t, dest, stdin, subcommand, args...)` — execute + JSON decode stdout
- `MustSucceed(t, stdin, subcommand, args...)` — assert exit code 0
- `MustFail(t, stdin, subcommand, args...)` — assert non-zero exit

`CommandResult` holds `Stdout []byte`, `Stderr []byte`, `ExitCode int`, `Err error`.

### 4. `e2e/testenv.go` — TestEnv (environment setup)
Layered factories:
- `NewTestEnv(t, agentName)` — bare temp dir with `ENTIRE_REPO_ROOT` + isolated `HOME`
- `NewKiroTestEnv(t)` — adds `.kiro/` and `.entire/tmp/` directories
- `NewKiroGitEnv(t)` — adds `git init`

Helper methods: `WriteFile`, `WriteJSON`, `ReadFile`, `FileExists`, `MkdirAll`, `GitInit`, `AbsPath`.

### 5. `e2e/fixtures.go` — Input builders
- `HookInput` struct with `.JSON()` method for building stdin payloads
- `KiroTranscript` builder with fluent API: `NewKiroTranscript(id).AddPrompt("...").AddPromptWithFileEdit("...", path).AddResponse("...", "...").JSON()`

### 6. `e2e/kiro_test.go` — Full protocol coverage for Kiro
Tests for all 19 subcommands, organized by functional area:

| Area | Subcommands | Test Count |
|------|-------------|------------|
| Identity | `info`, `detect` (present + absent) | 3 |
| Session management | `get-session-id`, `get-session-dir`, `resolve-session-file`, `read-session`, `write-session` | 5 |
| Transcript | `read-transcript`, `chunk-transcript`, `reassemble-transcript` | 3 |
| Hooks | `parse-hook` (spawn, prompt-submit, pre-tool-use, stop), `install-hooks`, `uninstall-hooks`, `are-hooks-installed` | 6 |
| Transcript analysis | `get-transcript-position`, `extract-modified-files`, `extract-prompts`, `extract-summary` | 4 |
| Other | `format-resume-command`, error cases | 3 |

All tests use `t.Parallel()` and `t.TempDir()`.

### 7. Top-level `Makefile` (new)
```makefile
test-e2e:
    cd e2e && go test -tags=e2e -v -count=1 ./...

test-unit:
    @for dir in agents/entire-agent-*/; do $(MAKE) -C $$dir test; done

test-all: test-unit test-e2e
```

## Adding a New Agent

When someone creates `agents/entire-agent-foo/`:
1. TestMain auto-discovers and builds it — no registration needed
2. Create `e2e/foo_test.go` with `//go:build e2e`
3. Optionally add `NewFooTestEnv(t)` factory if agent needs specific dirs (e.g., `.foo/`)
4. Run `make test-e2e`

## Critical Files

| File | Why |
|------|-----|
| `agents/entire-agent-kiro/cmd/entire-agent-kiro/main.go` | Subcommand list — tests must cover all 19 |
| `agents/entire-agent-kiro/internal/protocol/protocol.go` | Handler signatures — defines flag names, stdin/stdout contracts |
| `agents/entire-agent-kiro/internal/protocol/types.go` | JSON response shapes — tests decode these |
| `agents/entire-agent-kiro/internal/kiro/agent.go` | Agent implementation — understand behavior to write assertions |
| `agents/entire-agent-kiro/internal/kiro/hooks.go` | Hook file paths — tests verify file-system side effects |
| `agents/entire-agent-kiro/internal/kiro/transcript.go` | Transcript format — drives fixture builder design |

## Verification

1. `cd e2e && go test -tags=e2e -v -count=1 ./...` — all tests pass
2. Each subcommand from main.go has at least one test
3. Tests run in parallel with no flakiness (temp dir isolation)
4. Adding a dummy `agents/entire-agent-test/` triggers auto-build in TestMain
